display.purpose: Hero masthead titles and giant numeral readouts. ClashDisplay Bold, clamp(3.5rem, 8vw, 6rem), tracking -0.035em, line-height 1.0. The biggest thing on the page by a factor of 2.

body.purpose: All body copy, form labels, table cells. Chillax Regular 400, 0.875rem/14px, line-height 1.5. Max 65ch for prose.

mono.purpose: Technical voice. System monospace, 0.6875rem/11px uppercase, tracking 0.14em. Silkscreened annotations on a control panel. Also used for code blocks and LCD readouts.

shadows.name: edge-raised
shadows.purpose: Convex bevel catch-light. Buttons, keycaps, knobs, raised panels, avatars at rest.

shadows.name: edge-inset
shadows.purpose: Concave recess shadow. Inputs, toggle tracks, inset panels, pressed buttons.

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Immediate feedback: hover color shifts, border changes.

motion.name: transition-normal
motion.purpose: Default state transitions: background changes, opacity fades.

motion.name: transition-slow
motion.purpose: Slower fills: progress bars, large area changes.

motion.name: transition-press
motion.purpose: Tactile press with slight overshoot. Toggles, button presses, switch levers. Simulates mechanical snap.

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Machined metal key with gradient face and edge bevel. The standard action button. Presses down on click.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid near-black button with subtle top catch-light. The bold alternative to accent. Use for primary CTAs.

components.name: Accent Button
components.description: Solid safety-orange signal button. The loudest button — use once per action group. Semibold weight.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Fully transparent, no border, no bevel. Gains a warm aluminium background on hover. For secondary/tertiary actions.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Recessed cutout with edge-inset bevel. Reads as an engraved label on a control panel. Focus adds an accent rim.

components.name: Toggle Switch
components.description: Hardware toggle with a machined metal thumb in a recessed track. Checked state fills the track with safety orange and snaps the thumb right with a mechanical overshoot curve.

components.name: Badge
components.description: Inline 20px-tall technical label. Mono uppercase, brushed aluminium background, hairline border. Reads as a silkscreened tag on a control panel.

components.name: Accent Badge
components.description: Solid safety-orange badge variant. For high-signal labels.

components.name: Panel
components.description: Standalone polished white surface with a hairline seam border and subtle edge-raised bevel. The standard container surface.
components.html:
Panel Title

Content goes here. Panels are joined by 1px seams, never by shadows.

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Sticky top navigation bar with a bottom seam border. Links in secondary ink, active link in primary ink. Includes mobile hamburger menu pattern.
components.html:
BRAND
Docs Download

narrative.overview:
Enclosure is a design system for interfaces that feel like the front panel of a precision-machined modular synthesiser. The default theme is a light aluminium field — cool, bright, tool-like — with polished white cells joined by crisp 1px seams. A single loud accent signal (safety orange, #ff570f) cuts through the monochrome: primary actions, focus rings, the checked toggle, the glowing LCD readout. The dark theme inverts to a gunmetal console for night sessions, keeping the same hue character and the same accent pulse.

The system is driven by a seed architecture: six CSS custom properties control every color token through hsl() + calc() derivations. Change two color pickers, retheme the entire system. The seed is the API.

The personality is surgical, industrial, and tactile. Typography runs at extreme contrast: ClashDisplay set huge and tight for display numerals and headings, Chillax for body, and a system monospace stack for the tiny wide-tracked uppercase labels that carry the technical voice. Material simulation — brushed metal gradients, edge bevels that flip on press, fractal noise textures — sells the physicality without decoration.

This system explicitly rejects: floating cards, box-shadow elevation, gradients as decoration, glassmorphism, hero-metric SaaS templates, and anything that reads as a Bootstrap or Material Design app. The interface should feel like it was milled from a billet of aluminium and assembled on a workbench, not arranged in a design tool.
keyCharacteristics: Seed-driven: every color derives from 6 channel variables; the theme is programmable
Light-first: default is a bright machined aluminium field; dark is a gunmetal console
Seam grammar: 1px borders and tonal shifts separate surfaces; nothing floats
Metal materials: gradients, textures, and edge bevels simulate machined metal, not elevation
Accent signal: the single chromatic signal — loud, industrial, used on ≤10% of any screen
Extreme type contrast: enormous tight display against tiny wide-tracked monospace labels
Hardware controls: knobs, switches, faders, keycaps, toggles — the interface feels operable

rules.name: The Seed Rule
rules.body: Every color token derives from six channel variables (--nc-seed-h/s/l, --nc-accent-h/s/l). Never hardcode a color in a component; reference the token. To retheme, change the seeds.

rules.name: The Accent Rule
rules.body: The accent is a 100% saturated signal color. It never desaturates, never shifts hue dramatically, never goes pastel. Its loudness is functional, not decorative. Use on ≤10% of any screen.

rules.name: The Metal Finishes Rule
rules.body: The surface ramp has exactly four finishes: raw field, bare aluminium, brushed panel, polished white. Darker surfaces are recessed (inset, console); lighter surfaces are raised (panel, polished). If you need a fifth surface, you've misused one of the four.

rules.name: The Three-Face Rule
rules.body: ClashDisplay for display/headings. Chillax for body/buttons/operational text. Monospace for technical labels/annotations. Never swap.

rules.name: The Tight/Loose Rule
rules.body: The larger the type, the tighter the tracking. Display numerals at -0.035em. Body at 0. Labels at +0.14em. The contrast in tracking is as important as the contrast in size.

rules.name: The Bevel-Only Rule
rules.body: Box-shadow is permitted exclusively for edge bevels (--nc-edge-raised, --nc-edge-inset). No drop shadows, no ambient shadows, no glow-as-elevation, no blur-based depth.

rules.name: The Press-Flip Rule
rules.body: Interactive elements that use Edge Raised at rest must flip to Edge Inset on :active. The bevel reversal is the tactile confirmation — no ripple, no color pulse, no scale bounce.

rules.name: The Token-Only Rule
rules.body: Components reference CSS custom properties exclusively. No hardcoded colors, radii, spacing, or font stacks in component rules. A component that references a hex value directly is a bug.

rules.name: The Dark-Auto Rule
rules.body: Every component works in both themes without a single theme-specific selector. The [data-theme="dark"] block only redefines tokens, never components.

dos: Do separate surfaces with 1px seam borders (--nc-line, --nc-line-subtle, --nc-line-strong) or tonal background shifts. The seam is the system's primary spatial grammar.
Do use the accent color on ≤10% of any screen. A primary button, or a focus ring, or a checked toggle — never all three competing.
Do use the four aluminium finishes (raw field, bare aluminium, brushed panel, polished white) consistently. Brighter surfaces are closer to the user.
Do set inputs on the Recessed Well background (--nc-inset) with the Edge Inset bevel. The cutout IS the affordance.
Do use ClashDisplay for display numerals and headings, Chillax for body and operational text, monospace for technical labels. The three-face rule is non-negotiable.
Do flip Edge Raised to Edge Inset on :active for all pressable elements. The bevel reversal is the tactile confirmation.
Do use the seed channels to customize the theme. Change --nc-seed-h/s/l and --nc-accent-h/s/l on :root; every component follows.

Do keep transitions at 90–280ms. State changes feel immediate (90ms fast, 160ms normal, 280ms slow for fills). The tactile press transition uses a slight overshoot curve (120ms, cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)).
Do target WCAG 2.1 AA contrast. Etched Black on Polished White achieves ~13:1. Text on accent achieves ~10:1.
Do use the prefers-reduced-motion media query to collapse all transitions to 0.01ms.
donts: Don't use box-shadow for elevation. The only permitted shadows are the physical edge bevels (--nc-edge-raised, --nc-edge-inset). No drop shadows, no ambient shadows, no glow-as-elevation.
Don't use gradients decoratively. The metal gradients simulate machined surfaces. No gradient washes, no gradient text, no gradient backgrounds for visual effect.
Don't use glassmorphism, backdrop-filter blur, or transparency effects. The interface is opaque aluminium, not glass.
Don't add decorative motion. Transitions serve state changes only. No page-load choreography, no hover bounce, no scroll-triggered reveals.
Don't use rounded corners above 16px except for fully round elements (badge pills, avatars, knobs). The system is machined, not molded.
Don't hardcode a color value in any component rule. Every visual decision flows through CSS custom properties.
Don't place ClashDisplay in buttons, inputs, labels, or operational text. Display fonts in functional UI read as a branding mistake.
Don't use the accent color as decoration. It marks primary actions, current selection, focus, and the active signal. Nothing else earns it.
Don't add a fifth surface finish. The four-finish ramp plus recessed well and LCD cavity are the complete vocabulary.
Don't use Material Design patterns: no floating action buttons, no ripple effects, no elevation-based shadow hierarchy. If it looks like a Google app, it's wrong.
Don't use generic SaaS template patterns: no hero-metric dashboards, no icon-above-heading card grids, no purple-to-blue gradients. If it looks like every startup landing page, it's wrong.
Don't be deliberately raw or brutalist. Industrial means precision-machined, not crude. The system is considered and surgical, not confrontational.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, list items, or callouts. The alert component's 3px left border is the sole exception.
